summary: Re-cut the `explain` output's retired `package` noun to the surviving floor vocabulary and fix its stale package-era spec cites

per.section: Decision: every placement speaks the corpus's vocabulary — no synonyms

edit.description: `explain <member>` narration (read.rs:410-414) emits "Governing package: its `{kind}` kind binds the `{name}` package, whose clauses check it." — the retired `package` noun in user-facing output, which 50-distribution's no-synonyms Decision forbids (the surviving unit is a **floor**, an exported clause array — 10-contracts, the package-noun Decision's residue paragraph names exactly "diagnostics that teach the package vocabulary"). Re-cut the string to the floor/kind/clause vocabulary. Also fix `bound_package`'s doc cites (read.rs:149-162): "Decision: package binding is by artifact kind" (20-surface) and "a kind defaults to its own name as package" (40-composition) name sections deleted in the six-noun re-cut — replace with the live cite or drop. Internal fn name `bound_package` is code's own (depth rule); only the diagnostic string + cites are in scope.

The `PackageResolver` doc at builtin.rs:403 cites 20-surface "package binding is by artifact kind" — a section the re-cut deleted (verified absent from specs/). Fix the stale cite; leave the internal identifier untouched (code owns its names).
Snapshot carries the `Governing package` line — regenerate to the re-cut wording (`cargo insta accept` after the string change).
Same `Governing package` snapshot line (custom-kind fallback branch) — regenerate.

tests.asserts: the `why`/`explain` snapshots contain no retired `package` noun; the governing-floor line speaks the corpus vocabulary (floor/kind/clause)

acceptance: `temper explain <member>` names the kind's governing **floor** (not "package"), so no `explain` output uses a retired synonym (50-distribution, no synonyms); read.rs/builtin.rs carry no cite to a re-cut-away spec section.
notes: Last named residue of the package-noun retirement (10-contracts, the package-noun Decision): the conformance pass, conforms_to, and RequirementRow.package facet all shipped (PKG-NOUN-*); only the user-facing diagnostic vocabulary + stale cites remained. Disjoint from both parked entries (neither touches read.rs's diagnostic or builtin.rs's cite). Internal names (bound_package/floor_package/PackageResolver) are code's own — out of scope.

summary: Recut the requirement to `means·kind·required·clauses?·verifiedBy?` — retire count/unique/membership/degree facets for a clauses array, across engine + SDK + seam

per.section: Decision: set-scope demands are clauses — no noun enumerates the algebra

gate.reason: frame-scale and cross-language: the four facets live in compose.rs (Requirement + CountBound:105/Membership:158/DegreeBound:118) and sdk/contract.ts (count?/unique?/membership?/degree? at :83-86, re-verified live this tick), plus drift.rs RequirementRow columns and roster.rs (the set-scope check), and moving them onto a `clauses` array reshapes ClauseRow/engine dispatch to carry set/edge-scope predicates and bumps SEAM_VERSION on both sides (tests/emit.rs runs the real SDK). Needs a decomposition ceremony (John + session) to cut the serialized chain before parallel-safe slices, like GATE-READ-LOCK-DEMOLITION got. Also resolves whether reachability-as-clause (the REACH-DIAL replacement) and the held general-degree-band predicate ride this same clause-algebra growth.

edit.description: The Requirement interface enumerates `count?`/`unique?`/`membership?`/`degree?` (contract.ts:83-86) and lacks `clauses?` — the facet spelling 10-contracts rejects. Placeholder anchor; the ceremony scopes the full cross-language chain (compose.rs/drift.rs/roster.rs/engine.rs + sdk/contract.ts/declarations.ts + SEAM_VERSION).

acceptance: A requirement carries set-/edge-scope demands as ordinary clause values (each with its own severity/guidance/cite), not named facet fields; the SDK Requirement type reads `means·kind·required·clauses?·verifiedBy?`; the lock's requirement row carries no facet columns; SEAM_VERSION bumped in lockstep.
notes: Parked for a decomposition ceremony — the Rust facets in compose/drift/roster + the SEAM bump make it a serialized chain, not a single disjoint slice. Do not file the naive single entry. Downstream of it: reachability-as-edge-scope-clause (the REACH-DIAL replacement, deferred behind this).

summary: Ship the three acquisition channels — SDK npm package, engine binary pinned via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone assets, the plugin/bundle — fail-loud on a missing platform binary

gate.reason: needs the per-platform engine-binary release workflow build-out + John's decide-at-release calls (optional Apple notarization for the standalone mac binary, USPTO name screen) before the mechanical wrappers validate end-to-end. No marketplace/signing creds exist — a marketplace is a git repo + marketplace.json, community submission a free web form gated by `claude plugin validate` (verified 2026-07-06, code.claude.com/docs); npm creds done 07-05. SDK scope resolved (`@dtmd/temper` 0.0.3, single package, `(sdk-package-layout)`); channel 1's SDK foundation shipped

new.description: Build the per-target release binaries and publish them to the npm platform packages (channel 2: the engine binary pinned by the SDK via per-platform `optionalDependencies`, plus standalone GitHub release assets), and produce/publish the plugin bundle with its `marketplace.json` (channel 3). Channel auto-detected. Distinct from any CI *check* job, which is a documented two-line user-authored job (`temper check` + `emit --frozen` byte-compare), not a generated file (50-distribution).

edit.description: The root `package.json` today is the *private* flume control-plane manifest (`temper-flume-harness`, `private: true`). The npm distribution entry needs platform-specific `optionalDependencies` pointing at per-target prebuilt-binary packages plus a launcher that resolves and execs the platform binary, erroring loudly if none is installed (fail-loud, 50-distribution). Reconcile with the flume manifest rather than clobbering it. NB distinct from sdk/package.json (`@dtmd/temper`, already published — the engine pin is declared there, once).

acceptance: A stranger acquires temper via the SDK's pinned engine binary (per-platform optionalDependencies), standalone release assets, or the plugin/bundle (marketplace.json); a missing platform binary is an install error, never a silent skip (50-distribution 'Fail-loud delivery').
notes: Release infra, mostly outside src/; cargo gates can't validate npm/bundle publish; needs human creds. ch1 = SDK npm (`@dtmd/temper`, published 0.0.3); ch2 = engine binary via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone; ch3 = plugin/bundle via `temper bundle` + marketplace.json. Parked on human release creds + the engine-binary workflow. USPTO name screen due before launch.
